title: A. L. Singer
content:
{{Top|rwp}}
'''A. L. Singer''' was the author of books 13–15 in the [[Star Wars: Episode I Adventures (Scholastic)|''Episode I Adventures'']] series and 11–12 in the [[Star Wars Adventures (Scholastic)|''Star Wars Adventures'']] series. Books 11–12 were reprints of 13–14 of the ''Episode I Adventures''.

Singer is a pseudonym for the writer '''Peter Lerangis'''. "A. L. Singer" is an anagram of "Lerangis".

==Biography==
Peter Lerangis was born in Brooklyn, New York in August 1955. He moved to Freeport, New York for school, which made him a bit more creative when it came to writing fiction. The first writing he published was under a pen name for his school's newspaper. Later he majored in biochemistry at Harvard. After singing a cappella with the Harvard Krokodiloes, becoming their musical director, and participating in a few plays at the university, he entered law school. Later he left law school to pursue musical theater. He later got a job as an editor.

After marrying his girlfriend Tina deVaron, they had a son called Nick, which made him turn full-time to writing. He mostly focused on writing novels for children and adolescents. Lerangis won the Schneider Award in 2013 for his novel ''Somebody, Please Tell Me Who I Am''.
